compiled_state.title: [debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode]: open bugs associated with unmerged worktree sessions appear in ticket list -> skill wastes cycles on pre-landing bugs
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

When /dso:debug-everything enters Bug-Fix Mode, it should only process bugs that are relevant to the code currently on the worktree. Bugs filed during an in-flight session whose fix branch has not yet merged to main reference code that does not exist yet on the current worktree.

#### Actual Behavior

debug-everything pulls all open bugs from the ticket system and attempts to investigate/fix them regardless of which session created them. Bugs filed during a session whose worktree branch has not merged to main may describe behaviors in code that: (a) is already fixed on that branch but not yet visible here, (b) references context only present in that branch, or (c) was created as an artifact of that session's in-progress state. The skill wastes agent cycles and may produce duplicate or conflicting fixes.

Concrete example from 2026-04-28: debug-everything session on worktree-20260428-170727 picked up bugs filed by worktree-20260427-162140 whose branch has since merged (PR #11). The bugs were valid, but the scenario where bugs are filed for code in a branch that hasn't merged yet creates the same problem in general.

#### Suggested Fix (from user)

1. Add a `code_version` or `branch` field to the bug report template (create-bug/SKILL.md and bug-report-template.md) so bugs can be annotated with the git commit hash or branch name active when the bug was filed.
2. In debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode, before dispatching /dso:fix-bug for a ticket, check whether the bug's `code_version` is an ancestor of HEAD. If the bug's branch is not yet merged to the current worktree, skip it and add a ticket comment: "Skipped: bug's associated branch <branch> not yet merged to this worktree. Re-queue when branch lands."

#### Impact

Wasted agent cycles. Risk of incorrect fix attempts when the bug references code only present in an unmerged branch. Risk of duplicate fixes when both sessions independently fix the same underlying issue.

#### Reference

- plugins/dso/skills/create-bug/SKILL.md
- plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md (Bug-Fix Mode section)
- plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/bug-report-template.md

comments.body: Investigated (debug-everything 2026-04-28): Design approach: add a 'code_version' field to the bug report template capturing the git SHA at filing time. In debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode, before dispatching fix-bug for each ticket, check whether the bug's code_version is an ancestor of HEAD via 'git merge-base --is-ancestor'. If not, add a skip comment and continue. Changes needed: (1) create-bug/SKILL.md (add code_version to template), (2) shared/prompts/bug-report-template.md (add field definition), (3) debug-everything/SKILL.md Bug-Fix Mode (add ancestry check). Leaving open for dedicated sprint.
comments.author: Test
